herbs.name: Mint
herbs.usage: Used in teas, oils, and cooking

herbs.name: Chamomile
herbs.benefits: Calms nerves and helps with sleep

herbs.name: Burdock
herbs.benefits:
Regulate pathways and proteins that drive inflammation.
Protect cells from damage.
Boost immune cells that can help fight cancer and slow tumor growth.
Help regulate blood sugar and insulin levels.
Decrease body weight.
Limit the growth of bacteria and yeast.
herbs.usage: To cook burdock root, you'll first need to peel it. You can then fry, boil, or sauté it. Recipes you can try include: Japanese sautéed and braised burdock root.

herbs.name: Neem
herbs.benefits: The leaf extract is used to reduce tooth plaque and to treat lice. Neem contains chemicals that might help reduce blood sugar levels, heal ulcers in the digestive tract, prevent pregnancy, kill bacteria, and prevent plaque from forming in the mouth.
herbs.usage: Neem oil is a natural, organic pesticide for plants, typically used by mixing 1-2 teaspoons of cold-pressed neem oil and a few drops of mild liquid soap into 1 gallon of warm water. Spray it directly onto leaves (top and bottom) weekly to control bugs like aphids and spider mites, avoiding application in direct sun to prevent leaf burn.
